title: DoorDash Q1 2026: Record Orders, Revenue Miss, Stock Pops
text: DoorDash reported Q1 2026 earnings with mixed results: strong operational metrics but a revenue miss. The company's GOV rose 37% from a year ago to $31.6 billion, beating analyst estimate of $31.5 billion. Gross margin came in at 51.9%, ahead of the 51.6% estimate. However, quarterly revenue of $4.04 billion fell short of the $4.14 billion projection. Earnings per share of 42 cents versus 36 cents expected represented a significant beat on the bottom line. Total Orders increased 27% year-over-year to 933 million, with the company adding 201 million orders during the quarter. The mixed results were well-received by investors who focused on the company's operational efficiency and strong demand indicators. Continued product improvements and healthy consumer demand trends helped drive record membership signups, a new high for monthly active users, and strong consumer engagement across marketplaces. The stock response demonstrated that investors value the company's ability to drive order growth and operational efficiency improvements, even when faced with revenue headwinds.
